compiled_state.title: As a developer, I get automatic EAGAIN retry when running tests in parallel
compiled_state.author: Main
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Add EAGAIN detection and automatic retry logic to suite-engine.sh
**Why**: When parallel test execution hits OS process limits, tests fail with "Resource temporarily unavailable" even though the test logic is correct. Automatic retry at reduced parallelism recovers transparently.
**Scope**:
- IN: EAGAIN pattern detection in _process_completed_test, single retry with MAX_PARALLEL=1 exported, exit code 254 combined with stderr pattern as dual-condition gate
- OUT: Exponential backoff, flaky-pass tracking, record-test-status.sh changes (separate story)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, suite-engine's _process_completed_test detects EAGAIN patterns in captured stderr after non-zero exit and re-invokes the single test file with MAX_PARALLEL=1 exported as an environment variable, subject to the existing TEST_TIMEOUT ceiling
  ← Satisfies: "Suite-engine's _process_completed_test function detects EAGAIN patterns"
- When this story is complete, retry triggers only when both exit code 254 and EAGAIN stderr pattern match — exit code alone is insufficient
  ← Satisfies: "Exit code 254 combined with EAGAIN stderr pattern triggers retry/reclassification — both conditions must match"
- When this story is complete, if the retry passes, the result is recorded as passed (the authoritative status)
  ← Satisfies: "When suite-engine retries a test and the retry passes, the retry result (passed) is the authoritative status"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Dual-condition gate (exit code 254 + stderr pattern) must be tested for both conditions independently — ensure tests cover exit-code-only and stderr-only cases to confirm neither alone triggers retry
- [Maintainability] EAGAIN regex pattern will also be used in record-test-status.sh — consider extracting to shared constant

comments.body: Consideration: [Implicit Shared State] The resource_exhaustion string literal is referenced across ccb7-ce69, ea0c-08b0, and 861e-6dee with no shared constant. Define the canonical string in one location (e.g., a shared variable in deps.sh or a comment contract) so all stories reference the same source of truth.
comments.author: Main
